message:
fix(http): terminate URL authority at '?' and '#' per RFC 3986

The URL parser only split the authority from the path on '/', but
RFC 3986 Section 3.2 says the authority component ends at the first
'/', '?', or '#'. This caused URLs like `http://host?query` to
absorb the query string into the host field (producing host
"host?query") and lose the query entirely.

Fixed both ParsedUrl::parse() and parse_proxy_endpoint() to find the
authority boundary using all three delimiters.

All 71 existing HTTP client tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
